channel.title: Ninad Pathak
channel.description: Programmer-turned-writer helping DevTools and SaaS companies create technical content their engineering teams actually approve.

item.title: Memory for Voice AI Agents: What Text Chatbots Cannot Do
item.description: Voice AI agents live or die by how they manage memory across a real-time streaming pipeline. Text chatbots solve memory with RAG. Voice agents need something different.

item.title: How Memory Works in DeerFlow
item.description: A deep dive into the memory architecture of DeerFlow: layered context passing, session state files, sub-agent isolation, and how it compares to Letta, AutoGen, and CrewAI.

item.title: RAG vs Memory: What AI Developers Need to Know
item.description: Understand the fundamental differences between RAG and memory systems for LLM applications, when to use each, and how to combine them in production.

item.title: Memory Hierarchy in AI Systems: From Sensory to Semantic
item.description: How分层记忆架构帮助AI系统实现长期上下文、个性化和持续学习——以及为什么平坦记忆会失败。

item.title: Short-Term Memory for AI Agents: A Practical Guide
item.description: Context windows are not memory. Here is what every production AI agent engineer needs to understand about token budgets, overflow handling, and how short-term and long-term memory actually work together.

item.title: The BEAM Memory Benchmark: Why 1M Context Windows Are Not Enough
item.description: The BEAM benchmark reveals that LLMs fail catastrophically at retrieving facts from the middle of long contexts. Here is what the data actually shows, why it happens, and what matters for real deployments.

item.title: Context Windows vs Memory: Why They Are Not the Same Thing
item.description: A 1M token context window is not memory. Treating it like one is how you build expensive systems that still forget what they were doing last Tuesday.

item.title: State of AI Agent Memory in 2026
item.description: The memory stack for AI agents has exploded into a fragmented mess of competing approaches. Here is what actually works, what is still research, and why the next 18 months will sort the winners from the wreckage.

item.title: How Memory Works in Claude Code
item.description: A practical guide to understanding how Claude Code retains context across sessions, uses project files, and manages long-term memory for coding tasks.

item.title: How Memory Works in HyperAgents
item.description: A deep dive into how HyperAgents retain context across interactions, layer memory architectures, and handle session continuity in production.

item.title: AI Memory Management for LLMs: What Actually Works
item.description: A senior engineer's breakdown of what memory management for LLMs actually looks like in production: eviction strategies, KV cache management, importance-weighted retention, and why your agent keeps forgetting things.

item.title: Technical Writing for Engineers: The 80/20 Guide
item.description: Most engineering documentation fails for the same reasons. Here is what actually moves the needle.

item.title: LLM token budgets: a practical guide to cost control
item.description: Real numbers, real pricing, and concrete strategies for keeping your LLM spend predictable.

item.title: RAG Evaluation Metrics: What Actually Matters
item.description: A practical guide to RAGAs, recall, precision, and the metrics that separate production RAG systems from prototypes.

item.title: The 800ms Barrier: Profiling the Latency Chain of a Real-Time Gemini 3.1 Voice Agent
item.description: I built a sub-second latency voice assistant and profiled every millisecond of the Audio-to-Audio request/response loop on a MacBook Air M2. Here is the bottleneck analysis.

item.title: What Nobody Tells You About Error Handling in Production AI Agents
item.description: Hard-won lessons from running AI agents in production: the error patterns that actually break systems, and the patterns that fix them.

item.title: Context Engineering as Heap Management: Measuring Accuracy vs. KV Cache Eviction
item.description: VRAM is too expensive to waste on low-attention tokens. I benchmarked KV cache eviction strategies to treat LLM context like a managed heap, reaching 90% pruning with zero recall loss.

item.title: From 4 Minutes to 12 Seconds: A Quantitative Audit of the uv Package Manager
item.description: I benchmarked Astral uv against Poetry and pip on a 2026 AI dependency stack. Here is how Rust-native tooling reduces build times by 20x.

item.title: 100ms Vector Search in the Browser: PGlite vs. SQLite-vec Head-to-Head
item.description: I benchmarked PGlite and SQLite-vec on a MacBook Air M2 to find the fastest WASM vector database for local-first AI applications.

item.title: Beyond the Chatbox: Benchmarking Claude Code vs. Gemini CLI for Autonomous Repository Refactoring
item.description: I ran a head-to-head benchmark of Claude Code and Gemini CLI on an M2 Air (16GB), testing their ability to autonomously refactor legacy code and resolve race conditions.
